english: Glr. — sde-srid (sde srid) 1. province, kingdom Cs. 2. regent, administrator, in more recent times title of the sdé-pa (sde pa) of the Dalai Lama, and the rulers of Bhotan. Köpp. II., 154.
W. *ï-ru dé-če med* (*i ru de che med*) there is here no room any more.
(? _debs) ('debs) time, times, = lan (lan) W., e.g. four times.

pf. bsdebs (bsdebs), fut. bsdeb (bsdeb), imp. sdebs (sdebs), 1. to mingle, mix, blend (pyogs) (phyogs) gčig-tu (gcig tu) together, Lex., cf. sbyír-ba (sbyir ba). — 2. to join, unite, combine, drás-su sdéb-pa (dras su sdeb pa) Mil., by the context: sewed well together, — but drás-su (dras su)? — Gen. vb. n.: to join, to unite, dań (dang) with, also la (la), sems mig dań bsdebs-nas lta (sems mig dang bsdebs nas lta), rná-ba dań bsdebs-nas nyan (rna ba dang bsdebs nas nyan) Mil. the soul sees by joining the eye, it hears by joining the ear; to join company, to associate, to hold intercourse with, Mil.; also to have sexual intercourse Pth., cf. _dré-ba ('dre ba), _grógs-pa ('grogs pa), dzóm-pa ('dzom pa). — 3. to prepare, dress, get ready (victuals) Sch., cf. sbyór-ba (sbyor ba). — 4. to exchange, barter, truck for, *bág-pe ḍás-la* (*bag pe das la*) W. flour for rice; in this sense prob. also used by Mil.; to change, money, *nul deb sal* (*ngul deb sal*) please change me a rupee (not so in C.). — 5. to make poetry, to compose verses, at the end of poems: źés-pa ... kyis sdéb-pao (zhes pa ... kyis sdeb pa'o) the above verses have been composed by ...; = sbyór-ba (sbyor ba).
1. composition, esp. poetical, poetry, — 2. yi-gei sdeb-sbyór (yi ge'i sdeb sbyor) orthography Schr., Cs., Sch.
resp. gsol-sdér (gsol sder), dish, platter, plate, saucer; sder-góń (sder gong) a plateful; a dish (of meat etc.), esp. C.

claw, talon, sdér-kyu (sder kyu) Sch. id.; sdér-mo rno (sder mo rno) a sharp claw; sdér-čan (sder can) furnished with claws, sder-méd (sder med) without claws; sder-_dzin byéd-pa (sder 'dzin byed pa) to seize with the claws Cs.; stag(-gi)-sdér (stag gi sder) a tiger's claw Lt.; sder-čágs (sder chags) animals provided with claws Mil.

pf. (b)sdos ((b)sdos), fut. bsdo (bsdo), imp. sdos (sdos) (also dó-ba (do ba) q. v.) 1. to risk, hazard, venture, gen. c. dań (dang), also c. dat. or accus., bdág-gi lus (bdag gi lus) one's own body Dom.; lus srog dań (lus srog dang) frq., lus dań sróg-la (lus dang srog la) Dzl. — 2. to bear up against, sdug-bsńal, nyon-móńs-pa dań (sdug bsngal, nyon mongs pa dang), against heavy trials, against toil and drudgery Dzl.; to bid defiance, to an enemy Dzl., also to behave with insolence, contemptuously Dzl. — 3. lág-pas (lag pas) Dzl. 772, 6(?).

(C. vulg. *dúm-po* (*dum po*)) 1. trunk, stem, body of a tree Glr. — 2. stalk, of a plant, pádmai (padma'i) of a lotus; sdóń-po koń-stoń (sdong po kong stong) a hollow stalk Wdn.; sdóń-poi sde (sdong po'i sde) the class of stalked plants Cs. — 3. tree, also śiń-sdóń (-po) (shing sdong (-po)) frq.; śiń-sdóń rkań-gčig (shing sdong rkang gcig) a tree of a single stem Glr.; śiń-sdóń koń-rúl (shing sdong kong rul) a tree rotten at the core; col. fig. barren, of females, prob. jestingly. — 4. block, log. Comp. Cs.: sdar-sdóń (sdar sdong) trunk of a walnut-tree, śug-sdóń (shug sdong) stem of a juniper-tree; tsil-sdóń (tshil sdong) a tallow-candle; kyags-sdóń (khyags sdong) an icicle. — mčod-sdóń (mchod sdong) (Sch. = mčod-rtén (mchod rten)), in a botanical work it was explained by 'wick', = sdoń-rás (sdong ras), which seems to be more to the purpose, as a blossom is compared with it. — sdoń-rkáń (sdong rkang) v. sdoń-rás (sdong ras). — sdoń-dúm (sdong dum) stump of a tree, sdóń-dúm tsig-pa (sdong dum tshig pa) the burnt stump of a tree Cs. — sdóń-bu (sdong bu) Cs. 1. a small trunk. 2. stalk. 3. wick. — sdoń-rás (sdong ras), sdoń-śiń (sdong shing), sdoń-rkáń (sdong rkang) C. a wick of cotton, of wood, of pith; cotton wicks are used esp. for sacred lamps.

(Sch. also rdóńs-pa (rdongs pa)) pf. bsdońs (bsdongs), fut. bsdoń (bsdong), to unite, to join (in undertakings), to enter into a confederacy, to associate one's self with, c. dań (dang) (also accus.?); kyod dań ńa sdóń-ste _gro (khyod dang nga sdong ste 'gro) you and I, we will go together; sdóńs-zla (sdongs zla) prob. = zla-grógs (zla grogs).

pf. and fut. bsdad (bsdad), resp. bźés-pa (bzhes pa), W. *dád-če* (*dad che*), 1. to sit, frq., *sil-la dod* (*sil la dod*) W. sit down in the shade! dál-bar sdód-pa (dal bar sdod pa) to sit still Lt. — 2. to stay, to tarry, to abide, tóg-mar der bsdad (thog mar der bsdad) for the present I will stay here yet a little longer Mil.; nyál-nas bsdad-_dug-pa (nyal nas bsdad 'dug pa) to lie down and to continue lying Mil.; *dó'-du _júg-pa* (*do' du 'jug pa*) to receive hospitably, *mi _júg-pa* (*mi 'jug pa*) to deny reception, to send away C.; to stop, to halt, in running, walking Dzl.;
